title: TRIED Dataset Row
description: A single PyTorch-to-Triton translation example with all attempts, verification results, judge decisions, and final outcome. This is the record the orchestrator writes to the dataset after exhausting its retry budget (or succeeding earlier).

example_id.description: Stable UUID (v4) for this PyTorch op. Generated once when the example is first created. Shared across reruns of the same op; each rerun produces a new row carrying the same example_id.

attempts.description: Ordered list of generator-compile-run-judge cycles. Index in the array equals attempt_n.

final_winning_attempt_n.description: The attempt_n whose result is recorded in final_outcome. Null iff final_outcome is in {correctness_failed, all_attempts_failed}.

source.description: Immutable inputs that define this example. Copied verbatim from the corpus record (eval_and_training.json) when the agent loop starts.

pytorch_code.description: The PyTorch function or module source the generator is asked to translate. Must be standalone and executable without imports beyond torch.

origin.description: Provenance string. Format: '<source>/<name>'. Source is a repo name (e.g. 'flash-attention', 'mamba', 'torchao', 'tritonbench'), a model namespace ('hf', 'timm'), 'curated', or 'synthetic'. Examples: 'mamba/selective_scan_static', 'tritonbench/layer_norm', 'curated/softmax_fused', 'hf/bert-base-uncased', 'synthetic/fusion'.

input_shapes.description: Shape of each input tensor, as a list of integer lists. Example: [[32, 512, 512]] for a single 3D input.

input_dtypes.description: Dtype of each input tensor. Length must match input_shapes.

rng_seed.description: RNG seed used to generate input tensors. Fixed per example for reproducibility.

attempt_n.description: Zero-indexed position in the attempts array. Must equal the array index.

prior_advice_applied.description: The judge_fix_suggestion from attempt_n - 1 that this attempt acted on. Null on attempt_n == 0 or if the previous attempt yielded no suggestion.

triton_code.description: The Triton kernel source produced by the generator. Shipped to the verification server as-is.

compile.description: Result of the Triton compilation step on the verification server.

error.description: Captured compiler error text. Null iff status == 'success'.

correctness.description: Numeric agreement between candidate Triton output and reference outputs. Null iff compile.status == 'failed'.

benchmark.description: Timing measurements and speedups. Null iff correctness is null or correctness.status == 'failed'.

judge_fix_suggestion.description: Natural-language advice from the judge for the next attempt. Null when no further retry will happen or the judge declined to advise.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this attempt started (generator call kicked off).

correctness_check.description: Candidate-vs-eager (primary) and candidate-vs-Inductor (secondary) agreement. All 10 numbers recorded per benchmarking-protocol step 5.

status.description: 'passed' iff vs_eager is within the tolerance_policy_used.

correctness_stats.description: Five-number numeric agreement summary. Used in correctness.vs_eager and correctness.vs_inductor.

max_rel_diff.description: max(|a - b| / (|b| + eps)) over all elements. eps defined in tolerance.py.

n_elements_exceeding_tol.description: Count of elements that fail the tolerance policy.

pct_elements_exceeding_tol.description: n_elements_exceeding_tol as a percentage of total elements.

benchmark.description: Timing results per benchmarking-protocol.md: 10 warmup + 100 runs, CUDA event timing, synced start/end, median reported.

triton_ms.description: Median wall time of the candidate Triton kernel, in milliseconds.

eager_ms.description: Median wall time of PyTorch eager, in milliseconds.

inductor_ms.description: Median wall time of torch.compile + Inductor, in milliseconds.

speedup_vs_eager.description: eager_ms / triton_ms. >1.0 means Triton is faster than eager.

speedup_vs_inductor.description: inductor_ms / triton_ms. >1.0 means Triton is faster than Inductor.

triton_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 Triton timing runs, in milliseconds. Measures timing stability.

eager_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 eager timing runs, in milliseconds.

inductor_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 Inductor timing runs, in milliseconds.

latency.description: Per-stage wall times for this attempt, in milliseconds.

generator_ms.description: Wall time of the Ollama call that produced triton_code.

judge_ms.description: Wall time of the Azure judge call.

compile_ms.description: Wall time of POST /compile on the verification server.

run_ms.description: Wall time of POST /run on the verification server. Null if not reached (compile failed).

tokens.description: Token counts for the generator and judge calls in this attempt.

generator_prompt.description: Prompt tokens sent to the generator.

generator_completion.description: Completion tokens returned by the generator.

judge_prompt.description: Prompt tokens sent to the judge.

judge_completion.description: Completion tokens returned by the judge.

op_category.description: Coarse op family. Closed vocabulary. Finalized after corpus extraction.

final_outcome.description: Terminal state for the example. Closed vocabulary. Granularity is intentionally coarse; finer speed tiers should be derived from benchmark.speedup_vs_inductor in analysis rather than baked into the enum.

judge_classification.description: Judge's classification of an attempt's outcome. Closed vocabulary. The judge is constrained at call time to values in this set.

tolerance_policy.description: Key into packages/shared/src/shared/verification/tolerance.py. Closed vocabulary; that module is the source of truth for atol/rtol values and comparison semantics.
